quiz_questions.question_text: What is the difference between distance and displacement?

options.text: Distance has direction; displacement does not
They are the same thing
Displacement is always larger than distance
Distance is total path length (scalar); displacement is straight-line with direction (vector)

quiz_questions.explanation: Distance is a scalar that measures the total path length traveled. Displacement is a vector that measures the straight-line distance from start to finish, including direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is velocity?

options.text: Distance traveled per unit mass
Rate of change of displacement
Rate of change of distance
Rate of change of acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: Velocity is the rate of change of displacement with respect to time. It is a vector quantity that includes both speed and direction of motion.

quiz_questions.question_text: On a position-time graph, what does the slope represent?

options.text: Force
Velocity
Distance
Acceleration

quiz_questions.explanation: The slope of a position-time graph equals the change in position divided by the change in time, which is velocity. A steeper slope means faster motion.

quiz_questions.question_text: Can average velocity equal zero for a moving object?

options.text: Yes, if it returns to its starting position
Only at very high speeds
Only if it moves in a circle
No, never

quiz_questions.explanation: Average velocity = displacement / time. If an object returns to its starting position, displacement = 0, so average velocity = 0, even though the object was moving the entire time.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is instantaneous velocity?

options.text: The average of all velocities
Velocity measured over one hour
Velocity at a specific instant in time
The maximum velocity reached

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous velocity is the velocity of an object at a specific moment in time. On a position-time graph, it equals the slope of the tangent line at that point.

quiz_questions.question_text: How is average speed calculated?

options.text: Final speed minus initial speed
Displacement divided by time
Distance times time
Total distance divided by total time

quiz_questions.explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time. It is a scalar quantity and is always positive. Average speed is always greater than or equal to the magnitude of average velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: Is it possible for speed to be constant while velocity changes?

options.text: Only when acceleration is zero
Only at the speed of light
Yes, for example, in circular motion
No, speed and velocity are the same

quiz_questions.explanation: In circular motion, an object moves at constant speed but its direction continuously changes, so its velocity changes. Since velocity is a vector, a change in direction counts as a change in velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the SI unit for speed?

options.text: km/h
m/s
mph
cm/min

quiz_questions.explanation: The SI unit for speed (and velocity) is meters per second (m/s). Other units like km/h and mph are commonly used but are not SI units.

quiz_questions.question_text: Is distance always greater than or equal to displacement?

options.text: No, displacement is always greater
They are always exactly equal
Yes, distance is always greater than or equal to displacement magnitude
It depends on the mass of the object

quiz_questions.explanation: Distance (total path length) is always greater than or equal to the magnitude of displacement (straight-line distance). They are equal only when motion is in a straight line in one direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car travels 100 km in 2 hours. What is its average speed?

options.text: 200 km/h
50 km/h
100 km/h
25 km/h

quiz_questions.explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = 100 km / 2 h = 50 km/h.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the definition of 'distance' in physics?

options.text: The total length of the path traveled by an object regardless of direction
The straight-line measurement from the starting point to the ending point
The rate at which an object changes its position over a period of time
The change in velocity of an object divided by the elapsed time interval

quiz_questions.explanation: Distance is the total length of the path traveled, regardless of direction. It is a scalar quantity and is always positive or zero, never negative.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the definition of 'displacement'?

options.text: The total length of the path an object travels from start to finish
The straight-line distance and direction from start to finish
The speed of an object multiplied by the total time of travel
The force required to move an object from one place to another

quiz_questions.explanation: Displacement is the straight-line distance from start to finish, including direction. It is a vector quantity calculated as final position minus initial position.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'average velocity' mean?

options.text: The total displacement divided by the total time elapsed
The fastest speed reached at any point during the entire journey
The total distance traveled divided by the total time elapsed
The sum of all velocities divided by the number of measurements taken

quiz_questions.explanation: Average velocity = total displacement / total time. It is a vector quantity. Note that total distance / total time gives average speed (a scalar), not average velocity.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the difference between 'speed' and 'velocity'?

options.text: Speed is a scalar (magnitude only); velocity is a vector (magnitude and direction)
Speed is measured in m/s; velocity is measured in km/h exclusively
Speed applies to fast objects; velocity applies to slow moving objects
Speed can be negative; velocity is always a positive number or zero

quiz_questions.explanation: Speed is a scalar that describes only how fast (magnitude). Velocity is a vector that includes both how fast and in which direction. Speed is always positive; velocity can be negative (indicating direction).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'uniform velocity' mean?

options.text: Moving with constant speed and constant direction (no acceleration)
Moving with increasing speed in a straight line over time
Moving at the same speed as every other object in the area
Moving in any direction as long as the speed stays constant

quiz_questions.explanation: Uniform velocity means both speed and direction remain constant, so there is no acceleration. An object with uniform velocity moves in a straight line at a constant rate.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'position' mean in physics?

options.text: The location of an object relative to a chosen reference point or origin
The total distance an object has traveled since it started moving
The speed at which an object is currently moving through space
The force that keeps an object stationary at a fixed location

quiz_questions.explanation: Position describes where an object is located relative to a chosen origin or reference point. Displacement is the change in position (final position minus initial position).

quiz_questions.question_text: What does the term 'instantaneous speed' mean?

options.text: The magnitude of the instantaneous velocity at a specific moment in time
The average speed calculated over the entire duration of a journey
The fastest speed that an object is physically capable of reaching
The speed measured only at the very beginning of an object's motion

quiz_questions.explanation: Instantaneous speed is the magnitude of the instantaneous velocity at a particular moment. It is what a speedometer reads in a car and is always non-negative.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'non-uniform motion' refer to?

options.text: Motion where an object covers equal distances in equal time intervals
Motion where the velocity changes over time due to acceleration
Motion that occurs only in a perfectly straight horizontal line
Motion that happens when no forces are acting on a moving object

quiz_questions.explanation: Non-uniform motion occurs when velocity changes over time, meaning the object is accelerating or decelerating. An object in non-uniform motion covers unequal distances in equal time intervals.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'average speed' represent physically?

options.text: The constant speed that would cover the same total distance in the same total time
The speed at the exact midpoint of the journey measured by a speedometer
The arithmetic mean of the initial speed and the final speed of the trip
The speed that an object reaches just before it comes to a complete stop

quiz_questions.explanation: Average speed is the hypothetical constant speed that would allow you to cover the same total distance in the same total time. It equals total distance divided by total time.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the meaning of a 'negative velocity'?

options.text: The object is moving in the direction defined as negative (e.g., left or down)
The object is slowing down and will soon come to a complete stop
The object is not moving at all and is currently at rest in place
The measurement contains an error because velocity cannot be negative

quiz_questions.explanation: A negative velocity simply means the object is moving in the direction defined as negative by the coordinate system (e.g., to the left or downward). It does not mean the object is slowing down.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student walks 3 blocks north to school, then 3 blocks south back home. What is the student's total distance and displacement?

options.text: Distance = 0 blocks; displacement = 6 blocks north
Distance = 6 blocks; displacement = 0 blocks
Distance = 3 blocks; displacement = 3 blocks north
Distance = 6 blocks; displacement = 6 blocks north

quiz_questions.explanation: Distance is the total path length: 3 + 3 = 6 blocks. Displacement is the straight line from start to finish: since the student returned home, displacement = 0.

quiz_questions.question_text: A jogger runs 5 km in 30 minutes, rests for 10 minutes, then runs 3 km in 20 minutes. What is the jogger's average speed for the entire trip?

options.text: 10 km/h
8 km/h
16 km/h
4 km/h

quiz_questions.explanation: Total distance = 5 + 3 = 8 km. Total time = 30 + 10 + 20 = 60 minutes = 1 hour. Average speed = 8 km / 1 h = 8 km/h. Rest time is included in the total time.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car drives 60 km east and then 80 km north. What is the magnitude of the car's displacement?

options.text: 140 km
100 km
20 km
70 km

quiz_questions.explanation: East and north are perpendicular, so use the Pythagorean theorem: displacement = sqrt(60^2 + 80^2) = sqrt(3600 + 6400) = sqrt(10000) = 100 km. The total distance is 140 km.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student rides a bike at 10 m/s east for 5 seconds, then at 10 m/s west for 5 seconds. What is the student's average velocity?

options.text: 10 m/s east
0 m/s
10 m/s west
20 m/s east

quiz_questions.explanation: The student goes 50 m east then 50 m west, returning to the start. Displacement = 0, so average velocity = 0/10 = 0 m/s. However, average speed = 100 m / 10 s = 10 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A race car completes one lap around a 500-meter circular track in 25 seconds. What is its average speed and average velocity?

options.text: Average speed = 20 m/s; average velocity = 20 m/s
Average speed = 20 m/s; average velocity = 0 m/s
Average speed = 0 m/s; average velocity = 20 m/s
Average speed = 0 m/s; average velocity = 0 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Average speed = distance/time = 500/25 = 20 m/s. But after one complete lap, the car returns to its starting position, so displacement = 0 and average velocity = 0 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student walks 100 m north in 50 s, then 100 m south in 50 s. What is the student's average speed for the entire trip?

options.text: 0 m/s
4 m/s
2 m/s
1 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = (100 + 100) / (50 + 50) = 200/100 = 2 m/s. Even though displacement is zero, the average speed is not zero because distance was traveled.

quiz_questions.question_text: A delivery truck travels 30 km at 60 km/h, then 30 km at 30 km/h. What is the truck's average speed for the whole trip?

options.text: 45 km/h
40 km/h
50 km/h
35 km/h

quiz_questions.explanation: Time for first leg: 30/60 = 0.5 h. Time for second leg: 30/30 = 1 h. Total: 60 km in 1.5 h. Average speed = 60/1.5 = 40 km/h. The average speed is not simply (60+30)/2 because the truck spent more time at the slower speed.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a lab, a student rolls a ball that travels 2 m east in 1 s, then 3 m east in 1 s, then 4 m east in 1 s. Is the ball's velocity uniform?

options.text: Yes, because the ball moves in the same direction the whole time
No, because the ball covers increasing distances in equal time intervals
Yes, because the ball never changes direction during its motion
No, because the ball is moving to the east, not to the north

quiz_questions.explanation: The ball covers 2 m, 3 m, then 4 m in successive seconds, meaning its speed is increasing. Non-uniform velocity means the speed or direction (or both) is changing. Here the speed is increasing, so the ball is accelerating.

quiz_questions.question_text: A swimmer crosses a 50-meter pool in 25 seconds and swims back in 30 seconds. What is the swimmer's average velocity for the round trip?

options.text: About 1.8 m/s toward the starting end of the pool
About 1.8 m/s toward the far end of the pool
0 m/s because the swimmer returned to the starting point
2.0 m/s in the direction of the first lap of the swim

quiz_questions.explanation: After swimming to the far end and back, the swimmer returns to the starting position. Displacement = 0, so average velocity = 0 / 55 = 0 m/s. The average speed, however, is 100/55 = about 1.8 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A student throws a ball straight up at 10 m/s. At the very top of its path, what is the ball's instantaneous speed?

options.text: 10 m/s
0 m/s
5 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: At the very top of its path, the ball momentarily stops before falling back down. Its instantaneous speed is 0 m/s at that instant. However, it still has acceleration (9.8 m/s^2 downward due to gravity).
